conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for? If yes, could you please tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. My son Alex is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

To start, could you tell me: Is Alex currently in full-time education or approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is in sixth form doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. 

Could you tell me when Alex's course at sixth form is expected to end?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, he's doing a standard two-year A-level course, so he should finish next year when the academic year ends - around June or July.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. And can you confirm Alex's exact date of birth, or at least tell me if he has already turned 17 or will turn 17 soon?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's already 17 - he turned 17 a few months ago.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. A few more questions to complete the assessment:

1. Are you the person who is primarily responsible for Alex - meaning do you live with him and are responsible for his care?

2. Are you (or your partner if you have one) subject to immigration control, or do you have any restrictions on accessing public funds in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
1. Yes, I'm responsible for him - he lives at home with me.

2. No, I don't have any immigration restrictions or anything like that. I live in Manchester and there's no issue with public funds.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Just one final question:

Is Alex's course that he's currently doing considered "full-time" education? (Typically A-levels at sixth form are full-time, but I just want to confirm this is the case for Alex.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's full-time. He's there during the week doing his A-levels like any other sixth form student.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 17 years old and is in full-time approved education (A-levels at sixth form)
- Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim for children aged 16 or 17 if they're in approved full-time non-advanced education or training
- You're responsible for Alex and he lives with you
- You have no immigration restrictions preventing access to public funds

**Important note:** Your eligibility will continue until Alex finishes his A-level course (around June/July next year) or until the week after his 20th birthday, whichever comes first. Child Benefit payments will stop at that point unless there are other qualifying circumstances.

If you haven't already claimed Child Benefit, you can apply through the government website or by post. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is enrolled in full-time education (A-levels at sixth form). Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for a child aged 16 or 17 if they are in approved education or training. Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels) qualifies as approved education. Alex's course is expected to end next year (June/July), which means he is still within the eligible period. The claimant is responsible for Alex, lives with him in the UK (Manchester), and has no immigration restrictions preventing access to public funds. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (17 years old), who lives with them in Manchester. Alex is in full-time education doing A-levels at sixth form, which qualifies as approved education for Child Benefit purposes. The claimant has no immigration restrictions. Based on these circumstances, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
